title: lastrea filix-mas var. serratodentata
intro: Lastrea filix-mas var. serratodentata, also known as the toothed male fern or serra-toothed fern, is a variety of the common male fern (Lastrea filix-mas) plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the fern family (Dryopteridaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lastrea filix-mas var. serratodentata include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tinctures, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also sometimes used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of other health issues.
